title: Add OpenStack 2026.1 release config and Keystone tests
summary: Review of changes addressing sourcery-ai[bot] review #1 feedback: (1) ci.yaml Tempest matrix is now dynamically derived from releases/*/ directories instead of hardcoded, (2) verify_release_config.sh relaxed from cardinality checks (count >= 1) to schema/format checks (YAML tag == !!seq), allowing empty arrays. Both changes are correct, produce identical behavior to the previous implementation for the current release set, and introduce no new bugs.

tests_checklist.item: verify_release_config.sh passes for both 2025.2 and 2026.1 in CI (yq v4 tag function)
Dynamic matrix generation produces identical output to old hardcoded matrix
Slug-based directory discovery with fallback works for both 2025.2 and 2026.1
Schema validation (!!seq) correctly accepts empty arrays

code_quality_checklist.item: Inline matrix script is concise (25 lines) and well-commented with CC-0051 reference
verify_release_config.sh changes are minimal and targeted
No dead code or unnecessary changes
Consistent approach with build-images.yaml generate-matrix (both derive from releases/*/)

security_checklist.item: Release directory names are git-controlled (no user input injection)
JSON construction from directory names: malformed names cause jq parse failure (safe failure mode)

architecture_checklist.item: Dynamic matrix eliminates hardcoded release list — single source of truth in releases/
Schema validation is more forward-compatible than cardinality checks
No operator Go code changes required

fail_fast_checklist.item: jq -sc fails loudly on malformed JSON input
nullglob prevents literal glob if no releases/ directories exist
Empty matrix produces valid JSON that GitHub Actions handles (0 matrix entries = skip job)

defensive_checklist.item: shopt -s nullglob guards against empty glob expansion
Fallback to tests/tempest/keystone when slug-based directory doesn't exist
found_any guard in verify_release_config.sh catches missing release directories

suggested_improvements: The inline Tempest matrix generation script in ci.yaml (lines 109-133) could be extracted to hack/ci-generate-tempest-matrix.sh to follow the ci-script-extraction-with-env-var-interface pattern used elsewhere. This is cosmetic — the current inline approach is functional and small enough to be readable.
